body:value: The data refers to State/UT-wise details of persons injured and persons killed in accidents due to un-natural causes and total cases of un-natural accidents. Victims are categorised into male, female and total. The various causes of natural and un-natural accidents are categorized as Air-Crash, Collapse of Structure(House), Collapse of Structure(Building), Collapse of Structure(Dam), Collapse of Structure(Bridge), Collapse of Structure(Others), Drowning (Boat Capsize), Drowning(Other Cases), Electrocution, Explosion (Bomb explosion), Explosion(Other explosion like Boilers, Falls (Fall from Height), Fall into Pit/Manhole, Factory/Machine Accidents, Fire (Fireworks/Crackers), Fire (Short-Circuit), Gas Cylinder/Stove Burst, Other Fire Accidents, Fire-Arms, Sudden Deaths (Heart Attacks), Epileptic Fits/Giddiness, Abortions/Child Birth, Influence of Alcohol, Killed by animals, Mines or quary disaster, Food Poisoning/Accidental Intake of Ins, Spurious/poisonous liquor, Leakage of poisonous gases etc., Snake Bite/Animal Bite, Stampede, Suffocation, Traffic Accidents (Road Accidents), Traffic Accidents (Rail-Road Accidents), Other Railway Accidents, Other Causes and Causes Not Known.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Accidental Deaths and Suicides
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Home Affairs
Department of States
National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB)
field_sector:name: Home Affairs and Enforcement
Police
Statistics
title: State/UT-wise profile of accidents by un-natural causes

body:value: Session-wise details of disposal of notices of starred and unstarred questions of Rajya Sabha have been furnished in the data. The data elements includes category of questions, notices received, admitted questions, admitted by clubbing, disallowed, lapsed, returned, withdrawn and cancelled notices of questions (Rajya Sabha) . The Rajya Sabha or Council of States is the upper house of the Parliament of India. Members sit for six-year terms, with one third of the members retiring every two years.The Rajya Sabha meets in continuous sessions and, unlike the Lok Sabha, the lower house of Parliament, is not subject to dissolution.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rajya Sabha
field_ministry_department:name: Rajya Sabha
field_sector:name: Parliament Of india
Governance and Administration
Rajya Sabha
title: Session wise details of Disposal of Notices of Starred and Unstarred Questions of Rajya Sabha

body:value: The data refers to the area of forest and tree cover for States/UTs of India. Forest Cover refers to all lands more than one hectare in area, with a tree canopy density of more than 10 percent irrespective of ownership and legal status. Such lands may not necessarily be a recorded forest area. It also includes orchards, bamboo and palm. Tree cover comprises of tree patches outside the recorded forest area exclusive of forest cover and less than the minimum mappable area (1 ha).
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Environment and Forest
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Environment and Forest
title: States/UTs-wise Forest and Tree Cover

body:value: The another simple spacing method is use of condoms distributed at the district hospitals, sub-divisional hospital and community health centres to the beneficiaries under free distribution scheme and also sold by companies under social marketing scheme are recorded and reported in the Health Management Information System (HMIS) portal by States/UTs. The equivalent condoms users are calculated by dividing total number of condom distribution by seventy two during the year.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Family Welfare Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: State-Wise Achievements in Respect of Condom Users in India

body:value: The IUD Insertions is one of the spacing method adopted by the women to space the birth of children. The IUD Insertions done at the District Hospitals, Sub-divisional Hospitals and Community Health Centres during the reporting month are reported in the HMIS portal by all the States / UTs.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Family Welfare Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: State-Wise Achievements in Respect of Intra Uterine Device Insertions in India

body:value: The another spacing method is use of Oral Pill Cycles distributed at the district hospitals, sub-divisional hospitals and community health centres to the beneficiaries under free distribution scheme and also sold by companies under social marketing scheme are recorded and reported in the Health Management Information Systems (HMIS) portal by States/UTs. The equivalent oral pill users are calculated by dividing total number of oral pill cycles (packets) by thirteen.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Family Welfare Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: State-Wise Achievements in Respect of Oral Pill Users in India

body:value: Family Planning methods regulate the number and spacing of children in a family through use of contraceptives or other methods of birth control. The sterilization is one of method also known as terminal method to regulate the family size preferably after two or three children. The sterilization methods adopted by the eligible couples in all facilities, (SCs, PHCs, CHCs), sub-divisional hospitals, districts hospitals etc. are recorded / uploaded in the Health Management Information System (Portal) by all the States / UTs. The different procedure are adopted for undergoing sterilization by male(vasectomy) through No Scalpel Vasectomy (NSV)/Conventional Vasectomy and female (tubectomy) through laparoscopic sterilizations, mini-lap sterilizations and conventional operations & post partum sterilization done within 48 hours and 7 days of delivery.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Family Welfare Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: State-Wise Achievements in respect of Sterilisations in India
